title: Living in Boulder vlog 6 - 1 - 2017: Page counting for the classics

enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a late-night vlog update from Boulder about his ongoing project cataloging books on his website's education page, analyzing page counts, reading speeds, and book sizes for hundreds of classics. He briefly shares a historical tidbit about the Vestal Virgins of ancient Rome.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Exciting and worthwhile; comparable in effort to rebuilding a major project
Setting 1 minute per page as a standard estimate for comparing book sizes
Interesting historical fact worth sharing; their punishment for breaking vows was being buried alive

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He lives in Boulder, B-Lock.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He didn't do a video on June 1st because he was too busy with work and his website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He has been working on his education page on his website, adding books he has read.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He is organizing books into different sections of the menu and body on his website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He is figuring out how many pages the menus and body will have, and the percentage of the menu to the body.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He is calculating the average pages per chapter to understand the size of books.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He is excited to compare the sizes of different books because they are presented in the same style.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He has been listening to Momson, which is 3,200 pages long.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He set his reading speed to 50 seconds per page for his calculations.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: A word counter suggested it takes 53 seconds to read a page with 175 words.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He plans to make a chart to estimate how long it takes to read books of different sizes.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He wants to categorize books by size and create percentages for easier comparison.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He has started organizing his 303 books but hasn't done the same for his classics yet.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He forgot to include the dates that the authors lived on his website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He is committed to finishing this project and might work on it for weeks without stopping.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He compares this project to physically rebuilding realm on good words in terms of scale.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He mentions the festival virgins, female politicians whose job was to take care of the fire.

claims.subject: festival virgins
claims.claim: They were female politicians responsible for maintaining the fire in temples.

claims.subject: festival virgins
claims.claim: They served between the ages of six and ten until about 30 years old.

claims.subject: festival virgins
claims.claim: If they broke the law, like letting the fire go out, they could be punished severely.

claims.subject: festival virgins
claims.claim: Only ten of them broke the law in a thousand years.

claims.subject: festival virgins
claims.claim: Punishment included being buried alive with limited food and water.

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder, V-Lock for June 1st. Today's June 2nd. It is 1.35 in the morning right now.

Um, I, uh, let's see, what have I been, I don't think I did a video yesterday because I was too busy on my, uh, working on my website. And so, yeah, so what I've been doing on my website is, uh, I did attach a few pages to my education page.

I'm going to fill that page up with all the books I have. And so now I'm going through all those pages, those books, the ones that I have done and doing word counts on them, like on the different sections, the menu and the body and the different menus.

Sometimes I have different levels of menus with different detail and, uh, yeah, figuring out like how many pages the menu is, how much time it is, how many pages the whole thing is, how much time it is, the percentage of the menu to the body.
And then how many menus go in the body. How many chapters in the average page per chapter. To get a really good idea of, um, how big a book is, to be able to dissect it and figure it out.
Um, and to be able to compare the different books with each other because they're all, uh, presented in the same style.
So, uh, so, uh, it's super exciting for me because I'm curious to know how big these books are. Um, yeah. Um, and which ones are the really big ones?

Um, I've been listening to, uh, moms and just 3,200 pages. My, uh, the Apple, when it's set to fast reads at 50. Well, I, I timed it 48, uh, seconds to get through a page to 250 words. So I just set it at 50 for my math here. Um, and that's a good speed for me.

I think, uh, uh, uh, word counter says that it was 53 seconds for a page, 175 words in a page or a minute. Um, and, uh, I haven't done the test for the, uh, read aloud, the program on my cell phone yet.

Um, yeah, but, uh, I'll be just going to set it. I'm going to make a chart and set it for a minute, a page just to help get an idea, give an idea of, um, how long it takes to get through just different sizes of books.
Maybe even, yeah, percentages, like numbers. I just keep with this idea now. And then numbers of books in, in the different categories of size. So you get an idea too. That would be cool.
Um, that would be easy for me to make. So I already have my thing for my, I forgot he has three books, but not for my, uh, my classics.
Yeah. I'm putting all the classics on my website. So, um, um, yeah. And I also forgot to put, uh, the dates that the people lived.
So anyways, and then after that, I'll just finish up and make sure I get all of them. You know, I'm committed to this.
So I'll go for another couple of weeks without even blinking. Um, you know, and hopefully maybe I can be done by then.
But this is another one of those big projects, you know, like, I don't know, maybe even on par with physically rebuilding Rome on Google Earth. I don't know.
Anyways, I think I've talked enough.
Dun-dun-dun-dun-dun-dun-dun-dun-dun-dun.
I don't have anything historical to say because I can't think of it. I'm too high.
Yeah, I've been learning some interesting stuff. For example, maybe I'll do, like, one historical antidote video.
Um, the Vestal Virgins, that's in there. I thought that was interesting.
I mean, they were like the female, you know, politicians in the day. Their jobs were to take care of the fire.
When people run out of fire, they go to their temple and get more fire.
But they weren't supposed to. They were Vestal Virgins between 6 and 8, 6 and 10, and sort of 30 years.
And then they could be normal. But if they, like, broke the law, like, by having an affair or whatever.
And 10 of them did it in 1,000 years, they get very alive.
So they give them food and water.
And they just have to go down in the tomb and, like, live until they, you know, run out of oxygen or something.
Pretty crazy.
Um, so, okay.
That's that little antidote.
Okay, peace out.
